dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3391 IS: Accountability for Better Care Act of 2025
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Husted introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance

form.official-title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to extend and modify the enhanced premium tax credits, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Accountability for Better Care Act of 2025.

subsection.text: Subparagraph (E) of section 36B(c)(1) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended—

paragraph.text: by striking 2025 in the heading and inserting 2027,
by striking January 1, 2026, subparagraph (A) and inserting “January 1, 2028—

clause.text: except as provided in clause (ii), subparagraph (A)

paragraph.text: by striking the period at the end and inserting , and, and
by adding at the end the following new clause:

clause.text: in the case of such a year beginning after December 31, 2026, subparagraph (A) shall be applied by substituting 600 percent for 400 percent.

subsection.text: Clause (iii) of section 36B(b)(3)(A) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended—

paragraph.text: by striking 2025 in the heading and inserting 2027,
by striking January 1, 2026 and inserting January 1, 2028, and
by striking the last row of the table contained in subclause (II) thereof and inserting the following new rows:

row.entry: in the case of taxable years beginning before January 1, 2026, 400.0 percent and higher

subsection.text: Subparagraph (A) of section 36B(b)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended by adding at the end the following new clause:

clause.text: In the case of taxable years beginning after December 31, 2025, the initial and final premium percentages under clause (iii)(II) shall be adjusted such that in no case is the premium assistance amount greater than an amount equal to the monthly premiums described in paragraph (2)(A) with respect to the taxpayer, reduced by $5.

subsection.text: Subsection (e) of section 36B of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended by adding at the end the following new paragraph:

paragraph.text: In the case of a taxable year beginning after December 31, 2025, paragraph (1) shall be applied by substituting are not citizens of the United States for are individuals who are not lawfully present or, in the case of aliens who are lawfully present, are not eligible aliens.

subsection.header: Rules relating to coverage of abortion
subsection.text: Paragraph (3) of section 36B(c) is amended by adding at the end the following new subparagraph:

subparagraph.header: Rules relating to coverage of abortion

clause.text: A plan which provides any benefits or coverage for abortions shall not be treated as a qualified health plan.

Clause (i) shall not apply with respect to benefits or coverage of abortions where the life of the mother would be endangered if the fetus were carried to term, or the pregnancy is the result of an act of rape or incest.

subsection.text: The amendments made by this section shall apply to taxable years beginning after December 31, 2025.
Section 1402 of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (42 U.S.C. 18071) is amended by adding at the end the following new subsection:

There are appropriated, out of any monies in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, such sums as may be necessary for purposes of making payments under this section for plan years beginning on or after January 1, 2027.
Section 1402(e) of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (42 U.S.C. 18071(e)) is amended by adding at the end the following new paragraph:

paragraph.text: In the case of a plan year beginning after December 31, 2025, paragraph (1) shall be applied by substituting is not a citizen of the United States for is not lawfully present.
